title: CI: debian podman container checkpoint hang
body:
observed in integration tests quite a lot, possibly since the latest VM image update https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25181


```
# podman [options] run -d --network podman2 --name test_name quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest top
           2d8fe7c4a08e82ed9dd5ec317df0d0a6d9c9c24335e7d0c91f6d08e4f4930dea
           # podman [options] container checkpoint test_name
           SIGABRT: abort
           PC=0x486941 m=0 sigcode=0
         
           goroutine 0 gp=0x2f3c840 m=0 mp=0x2f3fdc0 [idle]:

```

Stack trace shows podman waiting for the oci runtime (crun) to exit. So crun seems to hang but to me it is more likely that the actual criu checkpoint hangs, either because of something in criu or the kernel.
This likely need to be reproduced outside of the test and then try to strace all the related process to see where it hangs.

So far I have only seen this on debian.

comments.body: I no longer see this since the last VM update
